schema_name: IPO Input Report — English — Schema A
description: Structured input schema for Exchange prospectus drafting, derived from the English IPO Input Report. Top-level categories mirror Report §1–§6; §1 is further broken into 12 sub-categories matching §1.1–§1.4(i).

categories.name_zh: 发行人必须提供的材料
categories.description: Materials the issuer must deliver to sponsors and reporting accountants. Organised into 12 sub-categories that mirror Report §1.1–§1.4(i).

fields.description: Original certificate evidencing the issuer's formation in its place of incorporation.
Business registration certificate issued by the local tax authority.
Current articles including all amendments approved by shareholders.
Registers of members, directors, secretaries, charges and minute books.
Board, audit/remuneration/nomination committee minutes covering the track-record period.
Current and historical register of shareholders.
Current register of directors and company secretary.
Complete record of share transfers, issuances, and reorganisations during the track-record period.
Description of the issuer's principal products and services as disclosed to regulators.
How the issuer generates revenue, including pricing, distribution, and go-to-market.
Itemised list of principal products/services with revenue contribution.
Key suppliers, manufacturing locations, and critical inputs.
Copies of contracts with the five largest customers and any customer exceeding 5% revenue.
Name, revenue, % of total, and length of relationship for each year.
Name, spend, % of total, and length of relationship for each year.
Revenue breakdown by geographic region across the track record.
Audited consolidated statements for the full track-record period.
Most recent interim period reviewed by the reporting accountants.
Monthly P&L/BS/CF used for stub-period cut-off testing.
Filed tax returns across the track-record period for each reporting entity.
Bank confirmations supporting the working capital and indebtedness statements.
12-month forward-looking working capital projection underpinning the sufficiency opinion.
Schedule of all borrowings, guarantees and contingent liabilities at the latest practicable date.
Planned capex over the forecast horizon, broken down by category.
Projection model supporting any profit forecast disclosed in the prospectus.
Summary of significant accounting policies, critical estimates and judgements.
CVs for every director, senior manager and key technical/operational personnel.
Completed D&O questionnaires and supporting background evidence.
Full disclosure of each director/officer's interests in shares, options and related entities.
Schedule of related-party transactions during the track-record period.
Signed conflict disclosures covering all competing businesses and outside directorships.
Appointment and resignation letters for directors during the track-record period.
Granted and pending patents with territory, status, and expiry.
Registered and pending trademarks with class, territory and status.
Software, content and other copyrighted material owned or licensed by the group.
In-licences and out-licences of material intellectual property.
Any pending or threatened IP litigation, oppositions or customs seizures.
All owned and leased properties with address, use, area, and status.
Title deeds, land-use rights certificates and real-estate registration.
fields.name_zh: 物业所有权/土地使用权证

fields.description: All material leases for the group's operating premises.
Valuation report prepared by an independent qualified valuer (required where §2 trigger applies).
Occupancy permits and construction completion certificates.
Environmental impact assessments, permits and regulator correspondence.
Material pending litigation or arbitration the group is involved in.
Concluded litigation during the track-record period with outcome and impact.
Any open inquiries, investigations or enforcement actions from regulators.
Letters exchanged with regulators during investigations.
Material settlement agreements arising from disputes or investigations.
Total employees by function/region at each period-end over the track record.
Service agreements for directors and senior management, including notice and non-compete terms.
MPF / pension scheme structure and contribution rates.
Any material labor disputes, collective bargaining or industrial action.
Terms of pre-IPO and post-IPO share option / RSU schemes.
Employment policies, code of conduct and related procedures.
List of all material insurance policies carried by the group.
Summary of insured risks, coverage limits and deductibles.
Insurance claims made and paid during the track-record period.
Directors' / sponsor's assessment that cover is adequate for the business.
All material operating licences and permits, with issuing authority and expiry.
Key compliance policies (AML, sanctions, anti-competition, etc.).
Correspondence with PRC regulators (CSRC, CAC, MIIT, etc.) where applicable.
Historical compliance breaches during the track-record period and remediation steps.
Anti-corruption policy, training records and whistle-blowing channels.
Compliance with PDPO, PIPL, GDPR or equivalent data-privacy laws.
Tax residency of each group entity.
Tax clearance certificates where obtained.
Open tax audits or disputes at the latest practicable date.
Contemporaneous transfer pricing documentation for material inter-company flows.
Key ERP, CRM, MES and e-commerce systems that support the business.
Information-security policy, access controls and incident-response procedures.
Material cybersecurity incidents during the track-record period.
Data-centre locations, cloud providers and hosting model.
Material outsourcing contracts for IT services and SaaS providers.

categories.description: Deliverables produced by the sponsor, reporting accountants, legal counsel and property valuer.

fields.description: Sponsor's declaration of independence and compliance submitted to Exchange.
Sponsor's undertaking to Exchange regarding continuing obligations.
Consolidated due-diligence memorandum documenting business, legal, financial and operational DD.
Indexed workpapers evidencing DD meetings, site visits, interviews and searches.
Analysis of the track-record period including management representations and sample testing.
Full accountants' report on historical financial information under Exchange App 1A.
Accountants' opinion on the adequacy of working capital for at least 12 months.
Statement of indebtedness at the latest practicable date for the prospectus.
Accountants' examination of any profit forecast included in the prospectus.
Comfort letters covering the long-form report, FIS, interim period, subsequent events and MD&A.
Comfort letter supporting the long-form historical financial information.
Comfort letter on the FIS cut-off, reconciliation and agreed-upon procedures.
Comfort letter covering the most recent reviewed interim period.
Bring-down comfort letter issued close to pricing on subsequent events.
Comfort letter on figures circled in the Management Discussion & Analysis.
HK counsel opinion on the due incorporation, good standing and corporate approvals.
HK counsel opinion confirming the absence of material undisclosed litigation in HK.
Verification notes evidencing every material statement in the prospectus.
Counsel opinion on incorporation and capacity of the Cayman/Bermuda parent.
PRC counsel opinion on regulatory compliance, CSRC filing and industry approvals.
PRC counsel opinion on validity of operating licences held by group entities.
PRC counsel opinion on legality and enforceability of contractual / VIE arrangements.
Counsel opinion on completeness and accuracy of prospectus disclosure.
fields.name_zh: 保荐人/包销商律师披露意见

fields.description: Rule 10b-5 disclosure letter on US securities-law disclosure where a US tranche exists.
Qualification of the independent valuer.
Independent valuation report prepared by a qualified valuer.

fields.field_name: Valuation Trigger Threshold (% of Total Assets)
fields.description: Threshold above which an independent property valuation report is required.
Maximum permitted age of the valuation date relative to prospectus date.
Primary format used to circulate working drafts.
Format used for tracked-changes / consolidated comments.
Format required for the final Exchange submission.
Whether both English and Traditional Chinese versions must be filed.
How redlines are generated, distributed and consolidated between rounds.
How clean versions are prepared and numbered for Exchange submission.
File-naming and versioning convention (e.g. v0.1, v1.0).
Approved VDR vendor for DD materials.
Encryption / password protocol for email transmission of sensitive drafts.
How physical signature copies are coordinated across counterparties.
Typical offset, in calendar days, of the all-hands kickoff relative to the target listing date.
Empirical minimum number of full prospectus-draft revisions across the deal lifecycle.
Whether printer must typeset English and Chinese versions in parallel.
Whether the verification workstream runs concurrently with drafting (vs. sequentially).
Expected minimum number of formal DD rounds (business / legal / financial).
Typical buffer between first printer typesetting round and PHIP filing.

categories.description: Per-section gating documents that must be in the VDR before the corresponding prospectus section can be drafted.

fields.description: Signed draft supporting the Cover and Parties Involved sections.
Business-DD memorandum underpinning the Business section disclosures.
Long-form accountants' report underpinning the Financial Information section.
Sponsor-maintained risk matrix feeding the Risk Factors section.
PRC counsel regulatory opinion underpinning the Regulatory Overview section.
Independent industry report underpinning the Industry Overview section.
Memo covering continuing connected transactions and waivers.
Memo describing the VIE / contractual arrangements, underpinning the VIE section.

categories.description: Exchange regulatory instruments and SFC guidelines that govern prospectus production. Every field records whether the issuer / sponsor has evidenced compliance.

fields.description: Formal sponsor appointment filing with Exchange.
Independent sponsor declaration (where applicable).

fields.field_name: Exchange Form M107 (Sponsor's Declaration on Filing)
fields.description: Sponsor's declaration accompanying the A1 filing.
Sponsor's undertaking of continuing obligations.
Exchange guidance on financial-information disclosure.
Exchange guidance on Waivers and Exemptions disclosure.

fields.field_name: HK Sponsor Due Diligence Guidelines (3rd Ed., March 2020)
fields.description: SFC/Exchange Due Diligence Guidelines 3rd edition, March 2020 — required reference standard.
Chapters of the Exchange Listing Rules governing the listing (typically Ch. 7, 8, 11, 19A).
